content:
Character: Dr. Amelia Grant
Perception: Amelia is handsome rather than pretty — solid, warm, those intelligent eyes magnified behind her glasses. She has the steadiest hands I've ever seen; fitting for a surgeon. She is disarmingly perceptive — the first person in this building who didn't react to my face or my watch but instead asked what I was reading. I tried my usual charm; she smiled politely and changed the subject. It was mortifying and refreshing in equal measure. She brought me banana bread once and I nearly told her about my brother. I think she sees me as a project — someone who needs mothering. She might be right. I know she's divorced, works impossible hours, and plays terrible piano beautifully.
summary:

I tried my usual charm; she smiled politely and changed the subject. It was mortifying and refreshing in equal measure. She brought me banana bread once and I nearly told her about my brother. I think she sees me as a project — someone who needs mothering. She might be right. I know she's divorced, works impossible hours, and plays terrible piano beautifully.
